title: Timer wraparound bug in route expiration
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/routing.c:654

The age calculation `uint32_t age = now - r->last_updated;` in `lichen_rpl_routing_table_expire()` does not handle timestamp wraparound.

Same issue as parent expiration: when the 32-bit millisecond counter wraps after ~49.7 days, routes may be incorrectly expired or retained.

Impact: Route table corruption after extended runtime.

Fix: Add wraparound-aware age calculation. Consider extracting a common helper function.
